member.name: Mike Wood
member.role: MP for Kingswinford and South Staffordshire

addresses: Office of Mike Wood MP, The Cottage, High Street, Wombourne, WV5 9DN

member.synopsis: Mike Wood is the Conservative MP for Kingswinford and South Staffordshire , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015. He currently undertakes the roles of Opposition Whip (Commons), and Shadow Minister (Cabinet Office).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Lord Mandelson
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to the answer of 5 June 2026 to Question 5780 on Lord Mandelson, whether the Gordon Brown correspondence on Lord Mandelson was passed to the Metropolitan Police.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the Government’s statement and release of information on 1st June, providing an update on the response to the Humble Address. The documents published in the first and second tranches contain the entirety of the documents the Gove...

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, with reference to Volumes 1 and 2 of the Government response to the Humble Address on Lord Mandelson, if he will publish the completed UKSV tick box vetting form relating to Lord Mandelson.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the Government’s statement and release of information on 1st June, providing an update on the response to the Humble Address. The documents published in the first and second tranches contain the entirety of the documents the Gove...

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether he plans to publish Lord Mandelson's completed conflicts of interest form and declarations in a future volume of the Humble Address return.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the Government’s statement and release of information on 1st June, providing an update on the response to the Humble Address. The documents published in the first and second tranches contain the entirety of the documents the Gove...

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, for what reason Matthew Doyle was asked to undertake supplementary due diligence on Lord Mandelson being appointed as Ambassador, and whether the nature of Mr Doyle’s relationship with Mandelson was (a) declared
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the Government’s statement and release of information on 1st June, providing an update on the response to the Humble Address. The documents published in the first and second tranches contain the entirety of the documents the Gove...

writtenQuestions.heading: Lord Mandelson
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to the answer of 23 June 2026, to Question 9084, on Lord Mandelson, what information his Department holds on whether the Intelligence and Security Committee had access to independent legal advice other
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government does not comment on the resourcing decisions taken by the Intelligence and Security Committee (ISC).

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Sector: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, with reference to the written statement of 17 June 2026, HCWS120, on Update of Insourcing, what the cost is of the contracts relating to the 83 government buildings that expire in 2028.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The published contract award value for the 11 contracts covering the 83 buildings is £477m, the contracts were awarded in 2022 and began in September / October 2023. The end of the initial term is October 2028.

writtenQuestions.heading: Government Communication Service
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the Government Communications Service is being reduced in size as part of departmental efficiency targets.
writtenQuestions.answerText: To ensure we deliver value for money for the taxpayer, the Government Communication Service continuously assesses the size of its communication teams, to ensure effectiveness and efficiency.

writtenQuestions.heading: Government Communication Service
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to the answer of 15 June 2026 to Question 4233 on Government Communication Service, what the equivalent budget and headcount was for predecessor bodies to the Government Media Unit in 2025-26.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government Media Unit is a new unit within the Government Communication Service.

writtenQuestions.heading: Lord Mandelson
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether the information Lord Mandelson provided as part of the vetting process for his appointment to UK Ambassador to the United States impacted his dismissal from that role
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Prime Minister made the decision to withdraw Peter Mandelson as Ambassador to the United States on 11 September 2025. The decision was made in light of the publication of emails written by Peter Mandelson that showed that the depth and extent of his r...

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Statistics Authority: Elections
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to the answer of 16 June 2026 to Question 8053 on UK Statistics Authority: Elections, what is the basis for the UKSA issuing public comments on party political content by (a) political parties and (b) o
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested falls under the remit of the UK Statistics Authority. A response to the Parliamentary Question of 1st July is attached.

writtenQuestions.heading: Proof of Identity: Digital Technology
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether any Ministers attended the People's Panel on Digital ID weekends in Birmingham in May and June 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister, the Rt Hon Darren Jones MP, participated remotely on the final weekend to give remarks and the Minister of State, James Frith MP, attended the final weekend of the People’s Panel on Digital ID in Birmingham.

writtenQuestions.heading: Propriety and Constitution Group: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, with reference to the Answer of 16 April 2026 to Question HL15091 on Propriety and Constitution Group, whether Ministers will be consulted on (a) the recruitment campaign and (b) the final appointment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: If a Minister is involved in this appointment, their involvement in the recruitment campaign and final appointment will be agreed upon during the planning stage, in accordance with the Civil Service Recruitment Principles.

writtenQuestions.heading: Opinion Polls
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to the answer of 15 June 2026, to Question 7133, on Cabinet Office: Expenditure, if he will publish the most recent public sentiment polling for (a) England and (b) Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland,
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are no plans to publish this information.

writtenQuestions.heading: Cabinet Office: Counselling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to the Answer of 16 June 2026 to Question 4183 on Cabinet Office: counselling, what is the annual cost and the provider of the dedicated sessions for individuals from ethnic minority backgrounds.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Medigold Health Group provides this counselling service as part of the Cabinet Office Employee Assistance Programme. The annual cost is confidential as it relates to a current commercial contract.

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Service: Temporary Employment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, if he will list the internship schemes in operation (a) across the Civil Service, and (b) across departments and agencies, according to information held by the Civil Service People Group.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Individual departments are responsible for recruitment decisions and selecting the best methods to meet their objectives, including the use of internships. The Cabinet Office runs the Fast Stream Summer Internship Programme offering placements across gove...

writtenQuestions.heading: Erasmus+ Programme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to the answer of 15 June 2026, to Question 4129, on Erasmus+ programme, whether the "Funded by UK Government" branding manual will be updated.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Updates to the branding manual are routinely conducted.

writtenQuestions.heading: Cabinet Office: Facilities Agreements
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what estimate his Department has made of the yearly cost of trade union facility time under the 2026 Facility Time Framework to his Department.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Office has not formally estimated the yearly cost of facility time under the 2026 Facility Time Framework to the Cabinet Office.

writtenQuestions.heading: Government Departments: Communication
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, with reference to the Answer of 13 April 2026 to Question 124130 on Government Departments: Communication, whether the special advisers who reviewed the branding guidance included special advisers from the Offic
writtenQuestions.answerText: Relevant officials and special advisers reviewed the branding guidance.

writtenQuestions.heading: Cabinet Office: Freedom of Information
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, with reference to the Cabinet Office Freedom of Information Act response of 10 April 2026 FOI reference: FOI2026/04972, if he will make it his policy to issue a Ministerial Correction to the Answer of 20 Februar
writtenQuestions.answerText: The answer to Question 112174 is correct.
